title: Telecommunications Line Installers and Repairers
description: Install and repair telecommunications cable, including fiber optics.

tasks.text: Set up service for customers, installing, connecting, testing, or adjusting equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Travel to customers' premises to install, maintain, or repair audio and visual electronic reception equipment or accessories.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure signal strength at utility poles, using electronic test equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect or test lines or cables, recording and analyzing test results, to assess transmission characteristics and locate faults or malfunctions.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Splice cables, using hand tools, epoxy, or mechanical equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Access specific areas to string lines, or install terminal boxes, auxiliary equipment, or appliances, using bucket trucks, climbing poles or ladders, or entering tunnels, trenches, or crawl spaces.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean or maintain tools or test equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: String cables between structures and lines from poles, towers, or trenches, and pull lines to proper tension.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Pull up cable by hand from large reels mounted on trucks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lay underground cable directly in trenches, or string it through conduits running through trenches.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Pull cable through ducts by hand or with winches.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Dig trenches for underground wires or cables.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Explain cable service to subscribers after installation, and collect any installation fees due.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place insulation over conductors, or seal splices with moisture-proof covering.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Compute impedance of wires from poles to houses to determine additional resistance needed for reducing signals to desired levels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install equipment such as amplifiers or repeaters to maintain the strength of communications transmissions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Use a variety of construction equipment to complete installations, such as digger derricks, trenchers, or cable plows.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fill and tamp holes, using cement, earth, and tamping devices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Dig holes for power poles, using power augers or shovels, set poles in place with cranes, and hoist poles upright, using winches.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
